title: Use purged race-time validation, then ensemble only stable models with light monotone calibration
summary: The remaining gains are small enough that selection bias is a real trap.
rationale: The remaining gains are small enough that selection bias is a real trap. Purged/embargoed validation is meant to prevent temporal overlap leakage, and stability-aware model selection is safer than chasing tiny CV lifts. For the final blend, rank averaging plus a light monotone calibrator is low-risk for ROC AUC.
implementation_hint: Evaluate candidates on race-ordered folds with a gap/embargo and track per-fold variance, not just mean AUC. Blend only models that stay strong under purged splits, average ranks or logits, then optionally fit beta calibration or ROC-regularized isotonic on out-of-fold predictions before submission.
expected_effect: May not raise single-model CV much, but should improve leaderboard transfer and reduce false discoveries from overfit feature tweaks.
risk: More conservative selection can look worse on naive CV; measure both naive and purged scores to quantify the gap explicitly.
